---
title: "Defining the diversity that impacts Alzheimer’s disease"
root: ".."
---

::: {.callout-note}
This page is a **scaffold**. Replace placeholder text with your own writing and add citations.
:::

## What “diversity” means in AD research

*Placeholder:* Define diversity dimensions relevant to prevention and diagnosis, e.g.:
- Genetic ancestry (and why it is not the same as race)
- Sex and gender
- Socioeconomic position and education
- Geography (urban/rural), health system access
- Environment, exposures, occupation, lifestyle
- Language and culture (testing and clinical interpretation)

## Pathways by which diversity impacts prevention and diagnosis

*Placeholder:* Map each dimension to mechanisms:
- Biological pathways (risk/resilience)
- Measurement / instrument bias
- Access to care and diagnostic pathways
- Representation in training/benchmark datasets

## A practical taxonomy

*Placeholder:* Provide a table of diversity dimensions and what variables should be reported/captured in datasets.
